_comment: Expiring permissions manifest for {{PROJECT_NAME}}. Permissions should rot loudly, not accrete silently. Every grant below has an owner and a reason; the whole manifest has an expiry.

renewal.policy: Re-review every 90 days. At renewal, DELETE every grant not used since the last review, then bump `expires` and append a changelog entry. Renewal is a deletion exercise, not a rubber stamp.

enforcement._comment: The expiry is inert unless something fails the build on it. Wire this gate up — an unenforced expiry date is decoration.
enforcement.gate: CI job runs on every PR and on a daily schedule; it parses `expires` and exits non-zero once the date is in the past, so the repo goes red on the day permissions go stale rather than years later.
enforcement.suggestedCi: test $(date -u +%Y-%m-%d) \< $(node -p "require('./.claude/permissions.json').expires") || { echo 'permissions manifest expired — re-review .claude/permissions.json'; exit 1; }
enforcement.onExpiry: Build fails. Fix by re-reviewing grants and bumping `expires` — never by widening the window without a review.

grants.permission: Bash(git status:*), Bash(git diff:*), Bash(git log:*)
grants.reason: Agent needs to read repo state to reason about changes.
Agent reads first-party source. Credential paths stay denied in settings.json.

changelog.change: Initial manifest. Baseline grants: read-only git + first-party source. Destructive shell, credential reads, and network exfil denied in .claude/settings.json.
